Title: User Registration
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the registration page.
2. User enters the username and password.
3. User clicks the "Register" button.
4. System validates the input fields.
5. System sends the registration request to the server.
6. Server generates an OTP and sends it to the user's email.
7. User receives the OTP and enters it into the system.
8. System verifies the OTP and saves the user details in the database.
9. System sends a confirmation email to the user.
Expected Result: User is registered successfully and receives a confirmation email.

Title: User Login
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login page.
2. User enters the username and password.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
4. System validates the input fields.
5. System sends the login request to the server.
6. Server checks the user credentials against the database.
7. Server generates an authentication token and saves it in the database.
8. System redirects the user to the home page.
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully and redirected to the home page.

Title: Password Recovery
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the password recovery page.
2. User enters the email address.
3. User clicks the "Recover Password" button.
4. System sends a password recovery request to the server.
5. Server checks the email in the database.
6. Server sends a password recovery link to the user's email.
7. User clicks the link and sets a new password.
8. System verifies the new password and saves it in the database.
9. System confirms the password change to the user.
Expected Result: User receives a password recovery link and successfully changes the password.

Title: Create Order
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the product page.
2. User selects the product and adds it to the cart.
3. User proceeds to checkout.
4. User enters the required details and clicks "Create Order".
5. System validates the order details.
6. System sends the order creation request to the server.
7. Server creates the order and saves it in the database.
8. System confirms the order creation to the user.
Expected Result: Order is created successfully and confirmed to the user.

4. System validates the update details.

Expected Result: Order is updated successfully and confirmed to the user.

Title: Delete Order
Test Steps:

Title: Swipe Card
Test Steps:
1. User opens the homepage.
2. User swipes n/2 cards.
3. Application fills the card with n objects.
4. Application requests more cards if remaining cards are less than n/2.
5. Server requests objects with a limit and receives them.
6. Database receives n/2 objects.
7. User continues to swipe cards.
8. Application fills the card with n/2 objects.
9. Server requests objects with a limit and receives them.
10. Database receives n/2 objects.
Expected Result: Cards are swiped successfully and filled with objects.

Title: Match Notification
Preconditions: Two users have swiped each other's cards
Test Steps:
1. User 1 swipes User 2's card.
2. User 2 swipes User 1's card.
3. Server tracks the object preference.
4. Server saves the preference in the database.
5. Server notifies both users about the match.
6. Users are directed to the match screen.
Expected Result: Both users receive a match notification and are directed to the match screen.

Title: Send Message
Test Steps:
1. User 1 composes a message.
2. User 1 sends the message.
3. Application 1 sends the message to Application 2.
4. Application 2 sends a notification to the server.
5. Server retrieves the message from the database.
6. Server sends the message to User 2.
7. User 2 receives the message.
Expected Result: Message is sent successfully and received by the recipient.

Title: Job Management
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the job management page.
2. User creates a new job.
3. System validates the job details.
4. System sends the job creation request to the server.
5. Server processes the job and triggers a push notification.
6. User receives the push notification.
Expected Result: Job is created successfully and a push notification is sent to the user.

Title: Job Loop
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the job management page.
2. User triggers a job loop.
3. Server processes the job and triggers a push notification.
4. User receives the push notification.
5. Server processes the job again and triggers another push notification.
6. User receives the second push notification.
Expected Result: Job loop is processed successfully and multiple push notifications are sent to the user.
